title: hieracium lachenalii subsp. dalicum
intro: Hieracium lachenalii subsp. dalicum, also known as Lachenal's hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion genus, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential remedy for wound healing and reducing fever. The health benefits of Hieracium lachenalii subsp. dalicum include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce blood sugar levels, and provide antioxidant protection. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal properties and health benefits.
